key_insight: Unsupervised computer vision pre-annotation can dramatically reduce the manual effort needed for high-resolution semantic segmentation in industrial materials science.
engineering_relevance: This approach speeds up expert labeling for microstructure datasets, making it more practical to build deployable deep-learning models for industrial inspection and characterization.
method: Use unsupervised algorithms to generate initial pixel-wise annotations.
Have experts correct the pre-annotated masks instead of labeling from scratch.
Train and validate a deep-learning segmentation model on the resulting public steel microstructure dataset.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a reduction in labeling time from 170 hours to 37 hours, corresponding to about a 78% decrease, on large 1280x959 and 960x703 images.
watch_item: Time savings may depend on image complexity, annotation standards, and how well the unsupervised pre-labels match the target microstructure classes.
paper_title: Speeding up the annotation process in semantic segmentation industrial applications
